element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Charge gauge mechanic for weapon damage and sprint
Leveled through repeated use; charged attacks unlock at skill level 2+
Player learns formulas from NPCs; requires ingredient pairs with no MP cost
Dog commanded to search ground for hidden ingredients in various environments
Currency changes per realm; conversion NPCs available
Throwable at range when leveled; replaces sword with more powerful variant
Requires extensive trading chains for rare items; shopkeepers specialize in goods
Area-of-effect charged attacks; replaces previous tier
Late-game weapon with limited ammo; Easter egg trigger if fully depleted
Advanced formula requirements tied to futuristic setting
Quick-access circular menu for equipment, alchemy, status checks

typical_relief_triggers: Resting at safe locations to restore HP
Discovering new alchemy formulas from Alchemists
typical_tension_triggers: Real-time combat encounters with hostile creatures
Challenging terrain requiring specific weapon types
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Cycles of exploration-combat-rest with escalating challenge per realm; tension builds through health attrition and enemy density; relief provided by safe zones and incremental power gains

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Out-of-the-way areas contain rare Alchemist NPCs and formulas; optional exploration rewarded
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Dog sniffing mechanic reveals hidden ingredient locations not visible on map; suggests player agency in discovery

difficulty_curve.early_game: Gentle introduction in Prehistoria with basic sword combat and simple alchemy; low enemy density
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Antiqua and Gothica introduce weapon swaps, complex trading chains, and increased enemy variety; moderate difficulty spike
difficulty_curve.late_game: Omnitopia presents advanced alchemy requirements, bazooka mechanics, and final boss challenges; highest difficulty
notable_spikes: Transition from Prehistoria to Antiqua (new weapon tier, trading system complexity)
Gothica mid-section (enemy density and realm-specific hazards)
Omnitopia finale (bazooka ammo scarcity, endgame alchemy formulas)

notable_level_patterns.description: Game organized as four distinct worlds (Prehistoria, Antiqua, Gothica, Omnitopia), each with escalating thematic complexity and mechanical depth; currency, weapons, and ingredients reset per realm
Specific weapons required to break environmental barriers; players must master current tier before accessing next area; encourages skill leveling within each realm
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Rock barriers in Antiqua requiring axe-tier weapons

notable_level_patterns.description: Rare formulas hidden in remote Alchemist locations; optional exploration rewards unique magic capabilities; ingredient availability tied to current realm ecosystem
Towns and caves act as relief nodes between combat zones; towns provide currency conversion, item trading, and equipment upgrades; spacing allows for measured progression
